facts.shortSummary: The session opens with the party laying out buffs, decoys, allied kobolds, and a ballista for a prepared ambush on the dragon.
facts.longSummary: After a brief framing recap and dramatis personae, the source reestablishes the party's plan for the dragon fight. Viepuck serves as the decoy, Celyn and the ballista are positioned apart from the main group, the friendly kobolds are kept farther back, and the whole setup is built around grounding the dragon and causing as much chaos as possible once it appears.

location.context: the prepared battlefield with the decoy, the ballista, and the allied kobolds set in separate positions
location.notes: The prepared source does not give a more specific place name for the dragon kill zone.

items.name: ballista
items.notes: The party has prepared a ballista specifically to bring the dragon down.

facts.shortSummary: The ambush turns chaotic, but the party crashes the dragon, kills it, and pushes the last hostile kobolds into surrender.
facts.longSummary: The dragon's first passes burn through the party's preparations, shrug off Izgil's magic, and force the fight into a hectic scramble around the battlefield. Celyn lands critical hits, Viepuck keeps the pressure on, Robin protects Izgil, and Robin's Greymalkin joins the aerial pursuit with a fly potion until the dragon finally falls. In the aftermath, one surviving enemy kobold makes a futile last charge and the kobold leader gives up under pressure from the party's allies.

location.context: the open battlefield where the dragon sweeps the party, the ballista crew, and the allied kobolds
location.notes: This beat stays at the same battle site established during the opening setup.

npcs.name: Greymalkin
npcs.notes: Greymalkin takes the fly potion and helps Robin intercept the dragon in the air.

npcs.name: kobold leader
npcs.notes: The leader surrenders after the dragon is brought down and the remaining enemies lose heart.

items.name: fly potion
items.notes: Robin gives the potion to Greymalkin for the aerial interception.

mainEnemies.name: dragon
mainEnemies.notes: Primary enemy of the opening battle.

mainEnemies.name: hostile kobolds
mainEnemies.notes: Additional enemies who appear at the edge of the fight and fold once the dragon dies.

combat.notes: The prepared ambush becomes a full battle and ends only when the dragon is dead and the remaining kobolds surrender.

facts.shortSummary: After the victory, the allied kobolds shower Greymalkin with tribute, the party gathers a little more intel, and then slips into the lair through a backdoor tunnel.
facts.longSummary: With the dragon down, the allied kobolds decide Greymalkin must be the true leader and ceremonially present him with a shiny belt buckle and more tribute. The party presses on quickly once it has the little extra information available, threading a backdoor tunnel toward the lair while Celyn scouts ahead, Izgil uses dimension door to get Robin through the tight spot, and the rest of the group rejoins inside after a brief stealthy scramble.

location.context: movement from the crash site into the backdoor tunnel and the first dark chamber beyond it
location.notes: The beat is defined by the transition from the outdoor battle area into the lair approach.

npcs.name: Greymalkin
npcs.notes: The allied kobolds briefly treat Greymalkin as the party's real leader after the dragon fight.

npcs.name: captive kobold
npcs.notes: The captive is intimidated during the tribute scene before the party moves on.

items.name: shiny belt buckle
items.notes: The allied kobolds award the buckle to Greymalkin as tribute.

facts.shortSummary: The party wipes out the remaining kobold defenders, clears the trapped tunnel, reaches the dragon loot, and ends by debating what to do with the dead dragon.
facts.longSummary: Once the disturbed guards return, the last real fight of the session proves brief and lopsided, with Celyn nearly deleting the kobold leader before she can act and Viepuck finishing the job. Afterward the party loots the barracks, works methodically through tripwires, spikes, and a grease trap in the tunnel, disarms the front-door trap near the treasure, and finally turns from clearing the lair to the practical problem of what anyone is supposed to do with a dragon corpse.

location.context: the barracks, trapped tunnel, and treasure area beyond the backdoor entrance
location.notes: The beat remains inside the lair from the renewed fight through the trap-clearing and loot.

npcs.name: kobold leader
npcs.context: spellcasting leader of the remaining defenders
npcs.notes: Celyn lands a devastating critical hit before she can take a proper turn, and Viepuck finishes her off.

items.name: dragon loot
items.notes: The party reaches the treasure after clearing the trapped approach.

mainEnemies.name: kobold leader
mainEnemies.notes: Leader of the remaining defenders inside the lair.

mainEnemies.name: remaining kobold defenders
mainEnemies.notes: The last hostile kobolds protecting the lair.

combat.notes: The lair fight is short and quickly gives way to trap-clearing, looting, and the post-battle cleanup.
